CONTENT.Club career: Born in Sant Pere de Vilamajor, Barcelona, Catalonia, Casadó is a youth product of CF Vilamajor, PB Sant Celoni, Granollers, and Damm. He moved to Barcelona's youth academy at the age of 13 in 2016. He was the captain of the Juvenil A team where he helped them win the league and Copa de Campeones in the 2020-21 season. Furthermore, he was on the bench for the reserve squad 5 times in 2021, and was promoted to the squad in the summer of 2022. On 5 July 2022, he extended his contract with the club until the summer of 2024.He made his senior debut with Barça Atlètic in a 3–2 Primera Federación win over Castellón on 27 August 2022. On 1 November 2022 he made his official debut for the senior squad against FC Viktoria Plzeň in the Champions League. On the 67th minute Franck Kessié got injured and Casado replaced him till the final whistle.In the 2023-2024 season, he was named captain of the Barcelona B team. He trained with the senior squad while playing games for Barcelona Atlètic. On 17 March 2024 he made his first LaLiga appearance against Atlético Madrid, where he got subbed on for Héctor Fort.
CONTENT.Playing style: Casadó is a defensive midfielder primarily, but has also played as right-back and centre-back. He is hard-working and adept at recovering possession, and is a great ball handler. He is a tenacious and intense player on the pitch.
CONTENT.External links: Marc Casadó at LaPreferente.com (in Spanish)Marc Casadó at BDFutbolMarc Casadó at Soccerway
CONTENT.International career: Casadó is a youth international for Spain, having been called up to the Spain U16s and U17s in 2019.

SUMMARY: Marc Casadó Torras (born 14 September 2003) is a Spanish professional footballer from Catalonia who plays as a defensive midfielder and captains Barcelona Atlètic.
